'''Ian Graham Knott''' (born 2 October 1976) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played for [[Wigan St Judes]] ARLFC, [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Wakefield Trinity]], and [[Leigh Centurions]] ([[Captain (sports)|captain]]),<ref name="They Played For Leigh (Statistics) at leighrl.co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/v2/they-played-for-leigh|title=They Played For Leigh (Statistics) at leighrl.co.uk|publisher=leighrl.co.uk|date=31 December 2019|accessdate=1 January 2020}}</ref><ref name="Heritage Numbers at leighrl.co.uk">{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/v2/club/heritage-numbers|title=Heritage Numbers at leighrl.co.uk|publisher=leighrl.co.uk|date=31 December 2020|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> as a {{rlp|fb|ce|so|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

Knott's career was ended in 2005 by a severe back injury sustained during a match,<ref>{{Cite web |date=2017-09-10 |title=Former Wire forward on how seeking help saved him from suicide after playing career was ended by injury |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/15521976.former-warrington-wolves-forward-ian-knott-speaks-out-on-world-suicide-prevention-day/ |access-date=2026-05-17 |website=Warrington Guardian |language=en}}</ref> which left him unable to walk before he underwent five surgeries and had a [[morphine pump]] implanted.<ref name="SOM">{{Cite web |title=Ian Knott – State of Mind |url=https://stateofmindsport.org/post/team/ian-knott/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20251116185504/https://stateofmindsport.org/post/team/ian-knott/ |archive-date=2025-11-16 |access-date=2026-05-17 |website=stateofmindsport.org |language=en-US}}</ref> He subsequently required the use of a [[walking stick]] and suffered from [[psychosis]] and [[Major depressive disorder|depression]], later revealing to ''[[The Guardian]]'' in 2019 that he still occasionally required the morphine pump and that he had attempted suicide following the injury.<ref name="SOMguardian">{{Cite news |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=2019-06-15 |title=State of Mind remains crucial for rugby league players and the public |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/jun/15/state-of-mind-mental-health-rugby-league-ian-knott |access-date=2026-05-17 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref> He now works as a public speaker for State of Mind, a charity that aims to tackle the stigma around the mental health of male athletes and their reluctance to discuss their struggles.<ref name="SOM" /><ref name="SOMguardian" />
